title: Medicine rationing ethics ledger
summary: Focused extreme medical support for medicine rationing ethics ledger in offline, delayed-help, or collapse conditions.
content: When medicines are scarce, secret decisions cause conflict and harm. A ledger creates transparency without giving untrained dosing advice. This is not medical advice; it is a safety and organization checklist until qualified help is available.
materials: clean water if available. Context: “Medicine rationing ethics ledger”.
soap or sanitizer. Context: “Medicine rationing ethics ledger”.
gloves or clean barrier. Context: “Medicine rationing ethics ledger”.
paper and pen. Context: “Medicine rationing ethics ledger”.
phone, radio, or messenger — for «Medicine rationing ethics ledger»
labels for time and symptoms — for «Medicine rationing ethics ledger»
steps: Inventory medicines by name, strength, owner, expiry, storage need, and prescription status.
Do not share prescription medicines without professional guidance.
Prioritize the medicine owner and life-sustaining continuity plans.
Use a small review group for scarce common supplies.
warnings: This is not medical advice and it is not a doctor's advice. It is offline emergency organization guidance only. Use local emergency numbers, trained first aid, pharmacists, clinics, doctors, poison centers, and official public-health instructions whenever available. Do not perform surgery, invasive procedures, diagnosis, prescription changes, or medicine dosing from this guide. Treat this as a specific boundary while managing “Medicine rationing ethics ledger”.

Escalate immediately for trouble breathing, heavy bleeding, unconsciousness, severe pain, chest pain, stroke signs, severe burns, pregnancy danger signs, infants in distress, poisoning, or rapid worsening. Treat this as a specific boundary while managing “Medicine rationing ethics ledger”.
Do not delay evacuation or professional care to finish a checklist. Treat this as a specific boundary while managing “Medicine rationing ethics ledger”.
Keep privacy, consent, dignity, and local law in mind even during collapse. Treat this as a specific boundary while managing “Medicine rationing ethics ledger”.
common_mistakes: Trying to diagnose instead of recognizing danger signs while carrying out “Medicine rationing ethics ledger”
Forgetting times, medicines, allergies, and changes while carrying out “Medicine rationing ethics ledger”
Touching fluids or wounds without hand hygiene while carrying out “Medicine rationing ethics ledger”
Moving an injured person without a safety reason while carrying out “Medicine rationing ethics ledger”
Letting panic, crowds, or rumors control care while carrying out “Medicine rationing ethics ledger”
alternatives: Use certified first-aid training where available as a fallback for “Medicine rationing ethics ledger”
Use official emergency services or the nearest clinic as a fallback for “Medicine rationing ethics ledger”
Use a pharmacist for medicine questions as a fallback for “Medicine rationing ethics ledger”
Use radio/phone consultation if transport is delayed as a fallback for “Medicine rationing ethics ledger”
Use written handover notes when devices fail as a fallback for “Medicine rationing ethics ledger”
failure_signs: Breathing, bleeding, consciousness, pain, fever, confusion, weakness, or skin color gets worse during “Medicine rationing ethics ledger”
The helper cannot keep the scene clean or safe during “Medicine rationing ethics ledger”
Transport, water, power, or communication failure blocks care during “Medicine rationing ethics ledger”
The person becomes unable to drink, speak, walk, recognize people, or stay awake during “Medicine rationing ethics ledger”
when_not_to_use: Do not use it to replace a doctor, ambulance, clinic, pharmacist, poison center, or trained first-aider when using the “Medicine rationing ethics ledger” record
Do not use it for surgery or invasive treatment when using the “Medicine rationing ethics ledger” record
Do not use it to change prescription medicines or doses when using the “Medicine rationing ethics ledger” record
Do not force care on a conscious adult except immediate life-safety actions allowed by local rules when using the “Medicine rationing ethics ledger” record
short_term: Reduce immediate danger, call or send for qualified help, keep the person clean, warm or cool as needed, and record facts without guessing. This note applies specifically to Medicine rationing ethics ledger.
long_term: Build certified first-aid capacity, update medical records, protect supplies, practice handovers, and map routes to pharmacies, clinics, hospitals, and shelters. This note applies specifically to Medicine rationing ethics ledger.
if_method_fails: Escalate by the fastest safe route: emergency number, clinic, hospital, pharmacist, poison center, radio medical consult, trained first-aider, or evacuation convoy. Record what failed and when. This note applies specifically to Medicine rationing ethics ledger.
environment_notes: In apartments, shelters, rubble zones, heatwaves, floods, smoke, and outages, medical problems worsen because of crowding, poor air, poor hygiene, no elevator, no refrigeration, and delayed transport. This note applies specifically to Medicine rationing ethics ledger.

title: Antibiotics no-sharing collapse rule
summary: Focused extreme medical support for antibiotics no-sharing collapse rule in offline, delayed-help, or collapse conditions.
content: Antibiotics can fail or harm when used for the wrong illness, dose, person, allergy, or duration. This is not medical advice; it is a safety and organization checklist until qualified help is available.
materials: clean water if available. Context: “Antibiotics no-sharing collapse rule”.
soap or sanitizer. Context: “Antibiotics no-sharing collapse rule”.
gloves or clean barrier. Context: “Antibiotics no-sharing collapse rule”.
paper and pen. Context: “Antibiotics no-sharing collapse rule”.
phone, radio, or messenger — for «Antibiotics no-sharing collapse rule»
labels for time and symptoms — for «Antibiotics no-sharing collapse rule»
steps: Do not use antibiotics for every fever, cough, or wound without medical guidance.
Record allergies and previous severe reactions.
Seek professional advice for infected wounds, severe illness, pregnancy, infants, immune weakness, and worsening symptoms.
Protect stored medicines from heat, moisture, and children.
warnings: This is not medical advice and it is not a doctor's advice. It is offline emergency organization guidance only. Use local emergency numbers, trained first aid, pharmacists, clinics, doctors, poison centers, and official public-health instructions whenever available. Treat this as a specific boundary while managing “Antibiotics no-sharing collapse rule”.

title: Pain medicine safety under stress
summary: Focused extreme medical support for pain medicine safety under stress in offline, delayed-help, or collapse conditions.
content: Pain, fear, and fatigue make overdose and dangerous mixing more likely. This entry keeps decisions tied to labels and professionals. This is not medical advice; it is a safety and organization checklist until qualified help is available.
materials: clean water if available. Context: “Pain medicine safety under stress”.
soap or sanitizer. Context: “Pain medicine safety under stress”.
gloves or clean barrier. Context: “Pain medicine safety under stress”.
paper and pen. Context: “Pain medicine safety under stress”.
phone, radio, or messenger — for «Pain medicine safety under stress»
labels for time and symptoms — for «Pain medicine safety under stress»
steps: Use only medicines the person can safely take and only according to label or clinician instructions.
Do not mix with alcohol or unknown sedatives.
Check duplicate ingredients in cold/flu products.
Record time and amount from the label without guessing.
Escalate if pain is severe, new, worsening, after trauma, chest/abdominal, or with confusion.
warnings: This is not medical advice and it is not a doctor's advice. It is offline emergency organization guidance only. Use local emergency numbers, trained first aid, pharmacists, clinics, doctors, poison centers, and official public-health instructions whenever available. Do not perform surgery, invasive procedures, diagnosis, prescription changes, or medicine dosing from this guide. Treat this as a specific boundary while managing “Pain medicine safety under stress”.
